title: santolina chamaecyparissus var. corsica
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus var. corsica, also known as Corsican gray santolina, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of essential oils such as camphor and borneol. The medicinal properties of Santolina chamaecyparissus var. corsica have been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, or by using the essential oil as a topical treatment.
